debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
C : Category α
X Y : α
f : Hom X Y
hm : @CategoryInContext.Category.Mono α C X Y f
he : @CategoryInContext.Category.split_epi α C X Y f
⊢ IsIso f

failed_attempts:
simp_all [comp_assoc]
      <;> try aesop
      <;> try simp_all [comp_assoc]
      <;> try aesop
    exact ⟨g, h1, h2⟩
  exact h

split epimorphism, there exists a g : Y → X such that f ≫ g = id X.
    -- This is the definition of a split epimorphism.
    exact he
  -- Now we have a g : Y → X such that f ≫ g = id X.
  obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h₁
  -- We need to show that f is an isomorphism, i.e., that there exists an inverse to f.
  -- We already have g such that f ≫ g = id X. We need to show that g ≫ f = id Y.
  have h₂ : g ≫ f = id Y := by
    -- Since f is monic, we can use the fact that if g ≫ f = id Y, then f is an isomorphism with inverse g.
    -- However, we need to prove that g ≫ f = id Y. We will use the fact that f is monic.
    -- We have f ≫ g = id X. We can try to show that f ≫ (g ≫ f) = f ≫ id Y.
    -- This would imply that g ≫ f = id Y if f is cancellable from the left (which it is, since it's monic).
    have h₃ : f ≫ (g ≫ f) = f ≫ (id Y) := by
      calc
        f ≫ (g ≫ f) = (f ≫ g) ≫ f := by simp [assoc]
        _ = (id X) ≫ f := by rw [hg]
        _ = f := by simp [one_coe]
        _ = f ≫ (id Y) := by simp [one_coe]
    -- Now we use the fact that f is monic to cancel it out.
    have h₄ : g ≫ f = id Y := by
      apply hm
      rw [← h₃]
      <;> simp [assoc]
    exact h₄
  -- Now we have g ≫ f = id Y and f ≫ g = id X. Therefore, f is an isomorphism with inverse g.
  refine' isIsoOfParallelEquiv ⟨g, hg, h₂, _⟩ <;> simp [h₂, hg]

simpa [one_mul] using hg
  have h_right : f ≫ g = 1 := by
    apply hm
    calc
      f ≫ g ≫ f = f ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [assoc]
      _ = f ≫ (1 : Hom X X) := by rw [h_left]
      _ = f := by simp [one_mul]
      _ = (1 : Hom Y Y) ≫ f := by simp [mul_one]
      _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ f := by
        calc
          (1 : Hom Y Y) ≫ f = f := by simp [one_mul]
          _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ f := by
            have h1 : (f ≫ g) ≫ f = f := by
              calc
                (f ≫ g) ≫ f = f ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [assoc]
                _ = f ≫ (1 : Hom X X) := by rw [h_left]
                _ = f := by simp [one_mul]
            rw [h1]
            <;> simp [one_mul]
            <;> try simp_all [assoc]
          _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ f := by rfl
  exact ⟨h_right, h_left⟩

so by monicity, g ≫ f = id'
    calc
      g ≫ f = g ≫ f := rfl
      _ = g ≫ f := rfl
  -- Conclude that f is an isomorphism with inverse g
  refine' ⟨g, _⟩
  ext
  <;> simp_all [hg, h₂]
  <;> rw [← comp_assoc] <;> simp_all [id_comp]
  <;> rfl
  <;> simp_all [comp_id, id_comp]
  <;> rfl

split epic condition
              obtain ⟨g', hg'⟩ := he
              have h7 : Epi f := by
                intro c h8 h9
                have h10 : f ≫ h8 = f ≫ h9 := by assumption
                have h11 : h8 = h9 := by
                  -- Use the fact that f is mono to deduce h8 = h9
                  have h12 : Mono f := hm
                  have h13 : f ≫ h8 = f ≫ h9 := by assumption
                  exact hm (by assumption)
                exact h11
              exact h7)
            <;> simp_all
          have h8 : Epi f := by
            intro Z h9 h10
            have h11 : f ≫ h9 = f ≫ h10 := by assumption
            have h12 : Function.Injective (fun g : Hom Y Z => f ≫ g) := h6 Z
            have h13 : (fun g : Hom Y Z => f ≫ g) h9 = (fun g : Hom Y Z => f ≫ g) h10 := by simp_all
            have h14 : h9 = h10 := h12 h13
            exact h14
          exact h8
        -- Use the fact that f is epi to deduce g ≫ f = id Y
        have h9 : Epi f := h5
        have h10 : Function.Injective (fun g : Hom Y Y => f ≫ g) := by
          apply Category.Epi_surjection f h9
        have h11 : (fun g : Hom Y Y => f ≫ g) (g ≫ f) = (fun g : Hom Y Y => f ≫ g) (id Y) := by
          calc
            (fun g : Hom Y Y => f ≫ g) (g ≫ f) = f ≫ (g ≫ f) := rfl
            _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ f := by rw [← assoc]
            _ = (id X) ≫ f := by rw [hg]
            _ = f := by simp
            _ = f ≫ id Y := by simp
            _ = (fun g : Hom Y Y => f ≫ g) (id Y) := rfl
        have h12 : g ≫ f = id Y := by
          apply h10
          exact h11
        exact h12
      exact h3
    exact h1

simp
          _ = id Y ≫ g := by simp
      _ = g := by simp
      _ = id Y ≫ g := by simp
  have h3 : IsIso f := by
    refine' ⟨g, _, _⟩
    · -- Show that g ≫ f = id Y
      exact h2
    · -- Show that f ≫ g = id X
      exact h1
  exact h3

simp
          _ = f ≫ id Y := by simp
      have h8 : g ≫ f = id Y := by
        apply h5
        <;> calc
          f ≫ (g ≫ f) = (f ≫ g) ≫ f := by rw [comp_assoc]
          _ = id X ≫ f := by rw [hg]
          _ = f := by simp
          _ = f ≫ id Y := by simp
      exact h8
    exact h4

simp
        _ = id Y ≫ g := by simp
        _ = g ≫ f ≫ g := by
          calc
            id Y ≫ g = g := by simp
            _ = g ≫ f ≫ g := by
              have h2 := congr_arg (fun x => x ≫ g) h
              -- Simplify the expression using the given retraction property
              simp [comp_assoc] at h2 ⊢
              <;> simp_all [comp_assoc]
              <;> rw [← h]
              <;> simp [comp_assoc]
    exact h1

simp [h₁, id_comp, comp_assoc]
            <;>
            simp_all [comp_assoc, id_comp]
            <;>
            aesop
          have h₈ : g ≫ f = id Y := by
            apply h₅
            simp [h₇]
          exact h₈
        exact h₆
      rw [h₃]
  exact h₂

have h : Epi f := by
    rcases he with ⟨g, hg⟩
    have h₂ : IsRetraction f g := by
      exact hg
    have h₃ : Epi f := by
      exact Category.retraction_epi f g h₂
    exact h₃
  exact ⟨h, hm⟩

so f is an isomorphism
  refine' ⟨g, _, _⟩
  · exact h₂
  · exact h₁

simp
            _ = id Y ≫ g := by simp
            _ = (g ≫ f) ≫ g := by rw [h₃] <;> simp [comp_assoc]
            _ = g ≫ f ≫ g := by
              simp [comp_assoc]
        exact h₄
      exact h₂
  exact h₁
